title: PlanModeState canonicalize TOCTOU + non-existent plan file fallback bypass
description:
Steps to reproduce:
1. 

Expected: 
Actual: 

Dimension: Security and Access (5) / Functional Correctness (1)

Location: src/session/state.rs:116-169 (is_tool_allowed_in_plan_mode).

Violation: Plan mode is supposed to restrict write_file to ONLY the plan file. The check uses fs::canonicalize on both target and plan_file (lines 137-139). If EITHER canonicalize fails, it silently falls through to string-based absolute-path comparison (lines 144-157). Three problems:

1. TOCTOU: fs::canonicalize resolves symlinks at check time. Between the check and the later write (in tools.rs write_file), an attacker (or a confused sub-agent) can replace the plan file with a symlink pointing elsewhere. The second check happens on a different filesystem state.
2. Non-existent plan_file: canonicalize fails when the target doesn't exist yet. The fallback path (lines 144-157) just does path-string comparison after joining with current_dir. An attacker who picks a cwd that makes './plan.md' resolve to the same absolute PATH STRING as the target file — even if they point to different inodes via symlink or case-insensitive FS (macOS default, Windows) — passes the gate.
3. std::env::current_dir() is read at check time and can differ from the cwd at write time (any tool that does std::env::set_current_dir between invocations breaks the invariant).

Mandated Refactor:
1. Compute the plan file's canonical path ONCE at plan mode entry, store the realpath in PlanModeState.plan_realpath. Reject plan mode entry if the plan file cannot be created/canonicalized.
2. In the allow check, require the tool arg path to canonicalize successfully AND equal plan_realpath. Refuse on any canonicalize failure — no string fallback.
3. At write_file execution time (not check time), re-verify the final resolved path against plan_realpath before opening the file.
4. Reject symlinks: std::fs::symlink_metadata and disallow if file_type().is_symlink().
5. Add tests for symlink swap, non-existent plan file, case-insensitive FS, relative-path confusion.
